title: How Investors Are Reacting To CleanSpark (CLSK) Using Bitcoin Mining Cash To Build AI-Ready Data Centers
text: CleanSpark is leveraging profits from its Bitcoin mining operations to fund expansion into AI-ready data center infrastructure. The company recently closed acquisition of a second Texas data center campus, adding 300 megawatts of power capacity. According to the article, CleanSpark has demonstrated rapid growth in Bitcoin mining output and expanded its hashrate to 50 EH/s. The strategic angle involves using cryptocurrency earnings to simultaneously build a Bitcoin treasury and reinvest in power-intensive computing facilities that can support high-performance workloads beyond mining. The data center expansion addresses a significant concern for shareholders: concentration risk in pure Bitcoin mining. By diversifying into AI and cloud computing infrastructure, CleanSpark is creating what analysts view as an important offset to dependence on volatile cryptocurrency prices. However, investors should recognize the inherent risk. The company's earnings remain heavily dependent on Bitcoin economics and mining rewards, creating earnings volatility. Simply Wall St notes that even with production scaling, CleanSpark's heavy reliance on Bitcoin prices and mining rewards remains a critical vulnerability. The platform's narrative projects $1 billion in revenue and $114.3 million in earnings by 2029, suggesting potential 58% upside to current valuations.
